task_completion.description: Agent views the Flight Plans in the Aerobridge admin panel to check aircraft and start/end times.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Web browser open to the Aerobridge Django admin panel (http://localhost:8000/admin/) showing the Flight Plans list or a specific Flight Plan's detail page.

task_completion.description: Agent views the Flight Logs in the Aerobridge admin panel to check aircraft and created_at times.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Web browser showing the Flight Logs list or a specific Flight Log's detail page, specifically looking for logs where the Flight Plan field is empty.

task_completion.description: Agent links the first orphaned Flight Log to its matching Flight Plan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Flight Log edit page showing a Flight Plan being selected from the dropdown, OR a green Django admin success message stating 'The flight log... was changed successfully'.

task_completion.description: Agent links the second orphaned Flight Log to its matching Flight Plan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second Flight Log edit page showing a Flight Plan being selected, OR a second green Django admin success message indicating another flight log was updated.

task_completion.description: Agent opens the unmatched logs report file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano, VS Code) or terminal is open showing the file /home/ga/Documents/unmatched_logs.txt.

task_completion.description: Agent appends the ID of the unmatched Flight Log to the report file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file /home/ga/Documents/unmatched_logs.txt contains an ID (likely a UUID or numeric ID) representing the third Flight Log that could not be matched.

integrity.description: Agent used a web browser to interact with the Aerobridge admin panel.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser (like Firefox or Chromium) navigating the Django admin UI, rather than the agent using curl, python scripts, or direct database queries to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: Agent actively compared Flight Plans and Flight Logs.
integrity.visual_evidence: Trajectory shows the agent switching between Flight Plan and Flight Log pages to check the aircraft and timestamps, rather than blindly guessing or running an automated script.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely updated the unmatched logs file with an ID obtained from the admin panel.
integrity.visual_evidence: The ID pasted or typed into the unmatched_logs.txt file matches an ID seen on the screen during the inspection of the Flight Logs in the browser.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Aerobridge admin panel is accessible at http://localhost:8000/admin/. The scenario contains two flight plans and three orphaned flight logs. Two of the logs should be successfully matched to the plans based on the aircraft and time criteria (Log's 'created_at' falls strictly between the Plan's 'start_time' and 'end_time'). One log will have no matching plan. The ID of this single unmatched log must be appended to /home/ga/Documents/unmatched_logs.txt.
